title: Shaken, Not Sorry 2.0: A One-night Bar Takeover
description: For one night, the bar transforms with bespoke cocktails, an exclusive snack menu, a guest DJ, and an atmosphere that invites you to stay a little longer.
longDescription:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026
8:00 p.m. – 12:00 a.m.
Dress code: Smart casual (no shorts please)
Reservation not required

For one night, the bar takes on a different energy. Bespoke cocktails, an exclusive Bar Snack Menu, a DJ setting the mood, and an atmosphere that invites you to stay a little longer. What happens after the first martini… is up to you.

Featuring
Kumar, The 2nd Floor Bartender
Guest DJ – DJ YuJin will be spinning a vibrant mix of house and disco, bringing his signature sound to the decks. He has performed at popular venues including Whisper Room, Madame Claude, Mondrian, and more.

Bespoke Cocktails
Happy Hour: 8:00 p.m. – 9:00 p.m.
Last orders at 11:30 p.m.

Kumar's Specials
Happy Hour: $15 | Regular: $18

Kumartini
Tanqueray Gin, Olive Brine & Cocchi Dry Vermouth

Barrel-aged Negroni
Tanqueray Gin & Cocchi Sweet Vermouth

French 75
Purple Imperial Gin, Citrus & Prosecco

Dirty Vesper Martini
Wheatly Vodka, Tanqueray Gin & Olive Brine

Martini No. 5
Wheatly Vodka, Cocchi Extra Dry Vermouth & Olive Stuffed Blue Cheese

Bar Snacks
8:00 p.m. – 11:00 p.m. (last orders at 10:45 p.m.)

STUFFED DATES $16
Pt Reyes blue cheese, prosciutto & hot honey drizzle

S&P FISH STREET TACOS $16
Tajin mangonada salsa, sunny-side-up quail egg & lime

ULTIMATE BLACK TRUFFLES GRILLED CHEESE BITES $18
Griddled sourdough, aged cheddar, candied thick-cut bacon, goat cheese & tomato bisque dip
Vegetarian version available upon request

TRUFFLE HOUSE FRIES $12
With umami aioli

BITE-SIZED SPICY FRIED STEAK FINGERS $18
Battered buttermilk, sriracha honey syrup, toasted sesame & spiked remoulade

title: Sundays Served Right @ The 2nd Floor

Unwind on Sunday with a relaxed semi-buffet lunch at The 2nd Floor. Enjoy a curated spread of starters, mains, and desserts crafted for a leisurely afternoon with family and friends.

For reservations and enquiries, contact The 2nd Floor at 6739 4329 or 2ndfloor@amclub.org.sg.

title: Tiny Art Explorers Program
description: Little artists explore fun, safe, and colorful art materials while bringing their imaginations to life through hands-on crafts. For kids aged 3–6 years old.
longDescription:
Thursdays, 3:00 p.m. – 3:45 p.m.
Registration not required
For kids aged 3 – 6 years old

Member: $3 | Guest: $5

Each week, little artists will explore a variety of fun, safe, and colorful art materials while bringing their imaginations to life through hands-on crafts.

Theme: Mother's Day
May 7: Mother's Day Card
May 14: Chalky Hearts for Mommy
May 21: Liberty Crown
May 28: Bald Eagle Paper Puppet

title: Friday Fun Night – May 2026
description: Family-friendly movie night with hands-on activities. May 22 features Detective Pikachu with a team-based Pokémon Trainer Bootcamp challenge.
longDescription:
May 8, 2026
6:00 p.m. – 9:00 p.m.

Movie: Encanto (1h 42m)

Activity: Sombrero Making
A fun, hands-on activity where kids create and personalize their own sombrero hats and assemble their own edible tacos.

May 22, 2026
6:00 p.m. – 9:00 p.m.

Movie: Detective Pikachu (1h 44m)

Activity: Pokemon Trainer Bootcamp
A team-based challenge where kids work in groups of five to tackle exciting Pokémon-themed missions.

Terms and Conditions:
- Registration required
- Min. 4 pax for the session to commence; max. 20 pax
- A confirmation email will be sent once the session is confirmed to proceed
- A 48-hour cancellation policy applies
- Registration closes 48 hours before the session. Sessions with insufficient sign-ups will be cancelled.

title: Take the Stage Together with Dad & Mom
description: A parent-child storytelling session led by a Singapore Repertory Theatre Teaching Artist where families bring stories to life through movement and play.
longDescription:
Sunday, May 24, 2026
2:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m.

Step into a world of storytelling as you and your child bring stories to life through movement and play. Led by a Singapore Repertory Theatre (SRT) Teaching Artist, this session sparks creativity, confidence, and connection.

Member: $80 per pair
Guest: $90 per pair

Register via the TAC Book app: Log into your TAC Book app > Explore > Leisure > Sessions > Quad > Events

Terms & Conditions: Min. 10 signups for the class to start.
reservation: Minimum 10 signups required for the class to proceed

title: Kids' Library Activities – May 2026
description: Celebrate Mother's Day with creative activities including a coloring contest and a heartwarming storytelling session about a mother's love and independence.
longDescription:
Celebrate Mother's Day with a creative craft activity and stand to win exciting prizes. Enjoy a heartwarming storytelling session about a mother's love and her son growing into independence.

"I Love You Mom" Creative & Coloring Contest
May 1 – 31, 2026
Thinkspace

Kids are invited to take part in our Mother's Day–themed coloring activity. Bring your creativity to life with vibrant colors and submit your masterpiece to Thinkspace for a chance to win exciting prizes.

One More Hug Storytelling
Saturday, May 30, 2026
10:30 a.m. – 11:00 a.m.

Chosen Title: One More Hug by Megan Alexander

Join a young boy as he navigates growing up with the comfort of his mother's reassuring hugs, building confidence along the way. Discover a heartfelt story of love, reassurance, and the enduring bond between parent and child.

title: TAC Book Club – May 2026
description: Join the book club discussion of "The Correspondent" by Virginia Evans, facilitated by Library Sub-Committee Member Kate Brundage. Complimentary event with light refreshments provided.
longDescription:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026
7:00 p.m. – 8:30 p.m.

Complimentary event; light refreshments provided
Facilitated by Library Sub-Committee Member Kate Brundage

Chosen Title:
The Correspondent by Virginia Evans

Sybil is seventy-three years old, in the winter of her life. She has always made sense of the world through writing letters, and through this epistolary novel, we see how she comes to terms with her past and present, and learns to forgive.

title: Art Launch: Beyond Tradition: A New Direction of Vietnamese Art By Art Blue
description: Explore Vietnam's contemporary art scene where tradition meets bold innovation. Vibrant artworks with intricate textures and daring colors invite viewers to discover fresh perspectives and engage with the creative process.
longDescription:
Art Exhibition at the Club:
May 5 – July 1, 2026

Art Launch:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026
6:00 p.m. – 7:30 p.m.

Step into Vietnam's contemporary art scene, where tradition meets bold innovation. Vibrant artworks with intricate textures and daring colors invite viewers to explore fresh perspectives, spark imagination and engage with the creative process.
